title: cephaelis remyana
intro: Cephaelis remyana, also known as the Queang or Remy's plant, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rubiaceae. It is a small evergreen shrub native to Southeast Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for centuries due to its medicinal properties. The plant's leaves and roots contain various bioactive compounds, including alkaloids and phenolic acids, which exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Cephaelis remyana include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and support the immune system, and it is often prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts for medicinal purposes.
